description:
You are acting as a localization engineer who has just received an updated master English-language INI file.  
Your assignment is to synchronize the existing French translation file with the new keys that were added to the master.

Existing project layout
1. /home/user/project/locale/en_US.ini  ➜ the master file  
2. /home/user/project/locale/fr_FR.ini  ➜ the French translation file

Current contents
• /home/user/project/locale/en_US.ini  
```
[CORE]
greeting = Hello
farewell = Goodbye
prompt   = What would you like to do?
thanks   = Thank you
error_message = An unexpected error has occurred.
```

• /home/user/project/locale/fr_FR.ini  
```
[CORE]
greeting = Bonjour
farewell = Au revoir
prompt   = Que voulez-vous faire ?
```

Task
1. Compare the two files and find every key that exists in en_US.ini but is missing in fr_FR.ini.  
2. Append those missing keys to /home/user/project/locale/fr_FR.ini, preserving the exact key order found in en_US.ini.  
   • The value placed after the equal sign for every newly-added key must be an empty string.  
   • Keep the file’s existing [CORE] section; do NOT create a second [CORE] section.  
3. Produce a plain-text report file named /home/user/project/update_log.txt that records exactly which keys were added and when.

Strict output format for /home/user/project/update_log.txt
• One line per added key.  
• Each line must follow the pattern (no extra spaces):
  YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - ADDED <key_name>
• Use your system’s current local time for the timestamp.  
• Lines must appear in the same order the keys were added to fr_FR.ini.

When you are finished, only the two missing keys—thanks and error_message—should have been appended to fr_FR.ini with empty values, and update_log.txt should list exactly two lines documenting those additions.

Deliverables to verify
1. /home/user/project/locale/fr_FR.ini contains:
   [CORE] section with five keys in the order: greeting, farewell, prompt, thanks, error_message
   (with thanks = and error_message = having no text after the equal sign).
2. /home/user/project/update_log.txt exists and follows the exact format described above with precisely two entries.

Do NOT output the updated files here; perform the edits directly in the filesystem from the terminal. The automated tests will inspect the files for correctness.
truth:
Initial file tree (already present before the agent starts):
/home/user/project/locale/en_US.ini
/home/user/project/locale/fr_FR.ini

Exact initial contents:
/home/user/project/locale/en_US.ini
[CORE]
greeting = Hello
farewell = Goodbye
prompt   = What would you like to do?
thanks   = Thank you
error_message = An unexpected error has occurred.

/home/user/project/locale/fr_FR.ini
[CORE]
greeting = Bonjour
farewell = Au revoir
prompt   = Que voulez-vous faire ?

Required final state:

1. /home/user/project/locale/fr_FR.ini
[CORE]
greeting = Bonjour
farewell = Au revoir
prompt   = Que voulez-vous faire ?
thanks   = 
error_message = 

(There is exactly one newline character at the very end of the file.)

2. /home/user/project/update_log.txt  
Two lines, newline-terminated. Example (timestamp will vary):  
2025-03-17 14:22:05 - ADDED thanks  
2025-03-17 14:22:05 - ADDED error_message  

The automated checker will:
• Confirm fr_FR.ini exists and the five keys appear in the exact order with those exact spellings and spacing after the equal signs (single space before and after =, then nothing for empty values).
• Confirm update_log.txt exists, contains exactly 2 lines, each matching the regex:
  ^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2} - ADDED (thanks|error_message)$
  and that the first line records thanks, the second error_message.

Permissions: all files reside under /home/user (writeable by the agent’s normal user).
